overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to computational social science, disinformation, and information operations. LinkedIn has the highest follower count and generally higher engagement per post. His content revolves around research publications, grant announcements, hiring opportunities, and conference presentations.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to ongoing research, publications, and methodologies in the field of computational social science.
Content focusing on the study, detection, and analysis of disinformation and information operations across social media platforms.
Posts announcing open positions for postdoctoral researchers and PhD students in relevant fields.
Announcements and updates related to presentations at academic conferences.

recommendations: Increase cross-promotion of content across different platforms to maximize reach.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to enhance engagement.
Engage more actively in conversations and respond to comments to foster a stronger community.
Use relevant hashtags consistently to improve discoverability.
Tailor content to the specific audience and platform to increase relevance.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows consistent engagement through likes and reposts, indicating a well-engaged audience interested in research publications and opportunities. Focus on sharing research outputs and community-building content.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has a smaller, but engaged audience. Content focusing on coordinated link sharing and methodological discussions seems to resonate. Continue sharing research updates and engaging in topical conversations.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn sees the highest engagement through likes and comments, particularly on posts announcing grants and hiring opportunities. Focus on professional announcements and opportunities, highlighting career advancements and research impact.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
